operation_names: Operation Iraqi Freedom (CENTCOM), Operation TELIC (UK), Operation FALCONER (Australia)
coalition_members: US (~148k troops), UK (~45k), Australia (~2k), Poland (~194); 49-country 'Coalition of the Willing'
children_killed_source_min: IBC ~6616 invasion-phase deaths, ~5% child proportion (Hicks et al. 2011), scaled to 20/42 days
us_weapons_documented: Mk-80 series bombs, JDAM, cruise missiles, cluster munitions, F-15E, F-16, B-2, B-1B, B-52, AH-64 Apache
cluster_munitions_used: Yes — 10,782 cluster munitions (~1.8M submunitions); HRW 'Off Target' 2003
key_incident_in_window: Shock and Awe: ~1700 sorties + 504 cruise missiles in first 2 days; all 50 decapitation strikes missed targets
ihl_documentation: US not party to AP I, CRC, Rome Statute, or CCM; Kofi Annan: 'it was illegal'
key_sources: Iraq Body Count; HRW 'Off Target' (2003); Lancet (Roberts 2004, Burnham 2006); Hicks et al. PLOS Medicine 2011

exact_name: War in Afghanistan / Operation Enduring Freedom
operation_names: Operation Enduring Freedom (OEF), Operation Freedom's Sentinel (OFS)
coalition_members: US (overwhelming majority), UK (cruise missiles opening night), Australia, Canada, France, Germany; Northern Alliance (Afghan ground forces)
children_killed_source_min: Demographic extrapolation: 300-500 civilians killed (low est.) x 40-50% child proportion
children_killed_source_max: Demographic extrapolation: 1000-1500 civilians killed (high est.) x 40-50%
children_injured_source_min: 2:1-3:1 injury ratio applied to killed estimates
us_weapons_documented: B-2, B-1B, B-52 bombers, Tomahawk cruise missiles, carrier aircraft, cluster munitions

ihl_documentation: UNAMA monitoring began 2007 only; Gen. Franks: 'We don't do body counts'
key_sources: Marc Herold 'Dossier on Civilian Victims'; HRW 'Fatally Flawed' (2002); Costs of War Project

exact_name: CIA Covert Drone Program in Pakistan

coalition_members: US CIA (primary); Pakistan ISI/military (targeting intelligence, airfield access, cover story)

children_killed_source_max: TBIJ / Pakistani media: 2 children among 5-8 killed in first strike

key_sources: TBIJ Pakistan database; Stanford/NYU 'Living Under Drones' (2012); Amnesty 'Will I Be Next?' (2013); The Intercept 'Drone Papers' (2015)

exact_name: Second Lebanon War / July War (Harb Tammuz)
operation_names: Operation Just Reward (renamed Change of Direction phases 1-11)
us_role: Primary weapons supplier; expedited delivery during active conflict
coalition_members: Israel (sole perpetrator); US as weapons supplier
data_quality: MEDIUM — analytical estimates from incident-level data
children_killed_source_min: Individually documented child fatalities in HRW incident-level reports
children_killed_source_max: 1/3 child ratio applied to estimated 700-900 deaths in first 20 days
us_weapons_documented: GBU-28 bunker busters (Raytheon — Bush admin expedited delivery), JDAM, CBU-87 cluster bombs, M26 MLRS/M77 submunitions, F-16I, F-15I, AH-64 Apache/Hellfire, M109 howitzers
cluster_munitions_used: Yes — ~4 million submunitions dropped on South Lebanon; ~1 million unexploded

ihl_documentation: UN Commission of Inquiry A/HRC/3/2; UNSCR 1701 ceasefire
key_sources: HRW 'Why They Died' (2007); HRW 'Fatal Strikes' (2006); HRW 'Flooding South Lebanon' (2008); Amnesty 'Deliberate Destruction' (2006)
